observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
the LCE direct the (a)
Inventory Committee to
(i) conduct a
comprehensive physical
inventory count covering
all the PPEs owned by the
Municipality pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2020-
006 dated January 31,
2020; (ii) intensify efforts
to obtain the needed
documents or information
for the recognition and
valuation of the donated
PPEs presented above,
otherwise record them in
accordance with the
provisions of COA
Circular No. 2020-006;
and (iii) coordinate with
the Municipal
Accounting Office for the
reconciliation of
accounting records with
the Report on the Physical
Count of PPE to address
the identified deficiencies
and variances in the PPE
account;
(b) Municipal Treasurer
and OIC-Municipal
Accountant, after this
exhaustion of all diligent
efforts, to evaluate the
PPE discrepancies if the
case would qualify for the
application of one-time
cleansing of PPE account
balances for the
derecognition of non-
existing/missing PPE
items under COA
Circular No. 2020-006,
provided that all the
requirements specified in
Items 7 and 8 thereof are
met;
(c) department heads to
account all donated PPEs
under their custody and
submit all pertinent
documents to the
Municipal Accounting
Office for the accurate
recognition in the books;
(d) OIC-Municipal
Accountant to
(i) recognize the donated
PPEs in the books of
accounts; and (ii) make
the necessary adjusting
entries to reflect the
correct balances of the
PPE account; and
(e) OIC-Municipal
Accountant, together with
the Municipal Treasurer
and Municipal Engineer,
to initiate the proper
recognition and inventory
of the Local Road
Network System with the
use of the drafted Local
Road Network Map of the
Municipality and other
relevant records; and
incorporate into the
standard operating
procedure the appraisal
costs of replaced road
networks whenever
improvements or
replacements are made to
any portion of the road
network system.
Further, we
recommended that the
LCE create a dedicated
committee that will
process the titling of the
parcels of land in the
name of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed that,
henceforth, in the recording of fines and penalties arising from real property tax,
records be kept in accordance with Sections 271 and 272 of the Local Government
Code and Section 23 of NGAS Manual.

Further, we recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to
make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected
accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal
Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor to furnish the OIC-Municipal
Accountant with a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount
due and collectible based on RPT Account Register/Taxpayers Index Card to provide an
accurate and reliable basis for the setting up of RPT and SET receivables at the
beginning of the year for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements;
and (b) instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant (i) to maintain RPT and SET
Receivables Subsidiary Ledgers and reconcile the accounts with the reported
delinquent real property taxes of the MTO so that the necessary adjustment(s) in the
books of accounts can be recorded to establish the correct balances of the accounts;
and (c) instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal
Treasurers Office, to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct
balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Inventory
Committee to conduct a physical inventory count of all PPEs at least once a year and
consider the process of complying with the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts
balances as authorized under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to
address the unreconciled discrepancies between the accounting and property records,
subject to the full compliance with the guidelines and procedures provided therein;
(b) Municipal Treasurer, along with the Municipal Assessor, to facilitate the
registration and titling of the identified parcels of land to establish absolute
ownership; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) reconcile the accounting records
with the RPCPPE to address the noted deficiencies; and (ii) make the necessary
adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the PPE accounts and their
corresponding accumulated depreciation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) OIC-
Municipal Accountant to (i) regularly verify and coordinate the status of all ongoing
projects recorded under the CIP accounts with the Municipal Engineering Office to
properly present their accumulated costs in the financial statements; and (ii) make
the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts;
and (b) Municipal Engineer to conduct an inspection of all ongoing projects at year-
end and instruct the contractors to submit progress billings, together with other
necessary documents, to ensure the proper recording of the completed works under
the CIP accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to (a) refrain from recording the procurement transactions in the books
of the Municipality prior to their actual delivery; and (b) prepare the required
adjusting journal entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Chairman of the
Local Development Council, direct the members and secretariat of the LDC, along
with the implementing departments, to work collectively towards ensuring the timely
completion of projects, thus, enabling the prompt realization of benefits for the
intended beneficiaries.

Further, we recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to inform
the Local Development Council for any changes in the National Tax Allotment of the
Municipality for their proper action.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct all implementing
departments to continue monitoring the implementation of programs and projects to
ensure the timely implementation/completion thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to (a) in the preparation of the
LDRRMF Investment Plan for the current year and prior years unexpended
balances, ensure that projects and activities included in the plan are specifically
described; (b) coordinate with the Municipal Planning Development Office to ensure
that all approved DRRM-related PPAs are included in the AIP; (c) monitor and
ensure the implementation of the DRRM-related PPAs; (d) ensure that the required
procedures for the changes in the annual budget are being followed and that these are
supported with valid authority and documentation, pursuant to Section 321 of R.A.
No. 7160; and (e) prepare the necessary reports and submit the same to the OCD,
DILG-LGOO, and Audit Team in accordance with the requirements of COA
Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive, in his
capacity as Chairman of the GFPS, direct the members of the GAD Technical
Working Group (TWG) and the GAD Focal Person to uphold the GFPS's
commitment to institutionalizing GAD mechanisms. This includes ensuring the
allocation of at least five percent (5%) of the Annual Budget to GAD-related
programs, projects, and activities, in compliance with the Magna Carta of Women
(Republic Act No. 9710) and other pertinent regulations.

Further, we recommended that the GAD TWG and the GAD Focal Person
maintain regular coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that all
submitted and maintained GAD reports and records are accurately prepared and
reconciled, reflecting true and fair financial positions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) as Chairman of the LSB,
instruct the School Board Secretariat, to monitor and ensure the submission and
posting of the SEF Annual Budget and SEF Utilization Reports, both quarterly and
annually, pursuant to Item 6.1 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s.
2017; and (b) instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the
Municipal Budget Office to ensure that accurate SEF Utilization Reports are
prepared and posted.

Further, to ensure proper implementation of projects, we recommended that the
Local Chief Executive, instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to (a) coordinate
with the implementing office/school heads concerned to facilitate the preparation of
accurate estimates and plans; and (b) conduct site inspection prior to the preparation
of program of work and plans and specifications and field monitoring on the progress
and development of the projects to ensure compliance with the required technical
specifications.

Moreover, we recommended that Local Chief Executive, through the Public
Schools District Supervisor, require the submission of an explanation on the noted
change of project at San Jose High School.

Lastly, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the
Municipal Engineering Office, (a) conduct a thorough investigation on the noted
undelivered items and require the submission of an explanation; and (b) if warranted,
request from the supplier the refund of the cost of the undelivered items.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer, as implementing department for
infrastructure projects, agreed to ensure the compliance of the contractors with the
posting requirements of COA Circular 2013-004.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Engineering Office to (a) closely monitor the implementation of all
projects; (b) remind the monitoring team to regularly assess the condition of
completed projects to detect defects early; and (c) require the contractor to perform
necessary repairs on defective portions of the projects at no additional cost to the
Municipality.

Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive, instruct the
Municipal Engineering Office to assess the negative slippage incurred by the
contractor on the Construction of flagpole with landscaping at the New Multi-
Purpose Building at Sta. Monica project and, based on the delay, require the
contractor to pay liquidated damages corresponding to the number of days delayed.

Moreover, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the
head of the Municipal Tourism Office to conduct a feasibility study to support the
operation of the municipal eco-park; and (b) request from the Sangguniang Bayan
the enactment of an ordinance that will govern the operation of the eco-park.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer agreed to (a) deposit all
collections intact and within the prescribed period; (b) prepare and submit the Report
of Checks Issued pursuant to the requirements of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to, together with other
concerned municipal offices, (a) strategize and exert more efforts to enforce the
collections of delinquent real property taxes which includes the conduct of intensive
tax campaigns and sending of Notices of Delinquencies; and (b) coordinate with the
Provincial Government for the enforcement of legal remedies pursuant to the
pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160 in order to persuade the taxpayers to pay their
taxes on time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BPLO and
Municipal Treasurer's Office (MTO) to (a) levy, assess and collect business taxes in
compliance with the Municipalitys Revenue Code, ensuring proper interpretation
and compliance with its provisions; and (b) apply the appropriate industry for the
categorization of business establishments; (c) require the submission of sworn
statement of gross receipts or sales and certified copies of income tax returns so as to
come-up with an accurate basis for the assessment of business taxes; (d) verify, assess
and subject the examination of books of accounts for the noted variance of
₱416,752.44 due to the under declaration of gross sales/receipts, and accordingly
demand the payment thereof; (e) ensure the imposition of penalties (surcharge and
interest) on late payments; (f) demand the collection/payment of the penalties totaling
₱139,191.01 pertaining to the penalties that should have been imposed on the two
business establishments; and (f) stop the imposition of business taxes for newly started
businesses based on their capital investments.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MTO and
other concerned offices to revisit the Revenue Code and update the necessary
provisions in order to (a) clearly express the intention of the Municipal Government
to arrive at higher business taxes; and (b) comply with the pertinent provisions of
BLGF Memorandum Circular No. 001-2020 dated January 2, 2020.

We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer to (a)
conduct a thorough review and evaluation of the Municipalitys existing
organizational structure to properly identify and establish the appropriate human
resource complement; (b) refrain from hiring job order personnel whose functions
overlap with those regular employees or are deemed recurring, necessary, and
essential to the Municipality; (c) consider creating casual or permanent positions and
appointing qualified JO personnel to fill vacant regular positions; (d) ensure that the
hiring of JO personnel is based on necessity of services, as well as their
qualifications and performance; (e) ensure that JO personnel are informed of the
details of the contracts; and (f) devise control mechanism to monitor the attendance
of the JO personnel, review the ARs submitted by JO personnel, ensuring that the
reported outputs are measurable and detailed, with adequate documentation to
support the services rendered.

We recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer
agreed to closely coordinate and strictly adhere with the laws, rules and regulations
pertaining to the reversion of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Human
Resource Management Officer to strictly comply with the provisions of the Omnibus
Rules on Leave by allowing leave monetization only for employees with at least fifteen
(15) days of accumulated vacation leave credits and ensuring that at least five (5)
days are retained after monetization; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the
lacking documentary requirements to ensure compliance with applicable rules and
regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal
Accountant agreed to (a) maintain compliance with the provisions on the regular and
full remittance within the prescribed period of the withheld taxes, GSIS, Pag-IBIG,
and PhilHealth premiums/contributions and loan amortizations; and (b) subject the
e-Konsulta benefits of the RHUs taxable permanent employees with the appropriate
withholding taxes as provided under BIR RMC No. 23-2012.

We recommended that
the Local Chief Executive
(LCE) instruct all
department heads and
designated supply
officers to (a) formulate
policies and procedures
for controlling and
monitoring of all the
purchased and issued
inventories; (b) ensure the
timely preparation and
submission of reports
pertaining to the issuance
of inventory items; and
(c) strictly comply with
the rules and regulations
outlined in the NGAS
Manual for the proper
recording, valuation and
preparation of the
required inventory
reports.
Further, we
recommended that the
LCE instruct the
Inventory Committee to
facilitate the conduct of
the physical count of all
inventory items
semestrally to establish
the correct balances for
each account and submit a
complete and accurate
Report on the Physical
Count of Inventory
(RPCI), as required.
observations.managementAction:
Recording of issuance of
inventories is conducted regularly
Inventory Committee conducted physical count of inventories on March 31, 2024

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and  the Local Chief Executive agreed to  instruct all department heads and designated supply officers to (a) formulate policies and procedures for controlling and monitoring of all the purchased and issued inventories; (b) ensure the timely preparation and submission of reports pertaining to the issuance of inventory items; and (c) strictly comply with the rules and regulations outlined in the NGAS Manual for the proper recording, valuation and preparation of the required inventory reports.

Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Inventory Committee to facilitate the conduct of the physical count of all inventory items semestrally to establish the correct balances for each account and submit a complete and accurate Report on the Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI), as required.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to  instruct the (a)Inventory Committee to (i) conduct a comprehensive physical inventory count covering all the PPEs owned by the Municipality pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; (ii) intensify efforts to obtain the needed documents or information for the recognition and valuation of the donated PPEs presented above, otherwise record them in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and (iii) coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office for the reconciliation of accounting records with the RPCPPE to address the identified deficiencies and variances in the PPE account; (b) Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant, after exhaustion of all diligent efforts, to evaluate the PPE discrepancies if the case would qualify for the application of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances for the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPE items under COA Circular No. 2020-006, provided that all the requirements specified in Items 7 and 8  thereof are met; (c) department heads to account all donated PPEs under their custody and submit all pertinent documents to the Municipal Accounting Office for the accurate recognition in the books; (d) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) recognize the donated PPEs in the books of accounts; and (ii) make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the PPE account; and (e) OIC-Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Engineer, to initiate the proper recognition and inventory of the Local Road Network System with the use of the drafted Local Road Network Map of the Municipality and other relevant records; and incorporate into the standard operating procedure the appraisal costs of replaced road networks whenever improvements or replacements are made to any portion of the road network system.

Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a dedicated committee that will process the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality.

We recommended and  the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office on the status of the projects recorded under the CIP account; (ii) regularly verify from the Municipal Engineering Office the status of all projects that needs to be recorded under the CIP account to fairly present the same in the financial statements; and (iii) make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts; and (b) Municipal Engineer to (i) conduct an inspection of all on-going projects at year-end and instruct the contractors to submit progress billing, together with other necessary documents, for the proper recording of the completed works in the Municipality’s books and (ii) properly record and validate the status of all projects of the Municipality in the Report on Government Projects/Programs/ Activities as of reporting date.

We recommended and  the Local Chief Executive, as the Chairman of the Local Development Council agreed to  direct the members and secretariat of the LDC, along with the implementing departments, to (a) continue enhancing the process of planning, executing, monitoring, and evaluating programs and projects prioritized under the 20% Development Fund to guarantee that the programs and projects are well-prepared and ready for implementation; and (b) aim for the timely completion of the projects through collective efforts to ensure the prompt realization of benefits for the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct all implementing departments to continue monitoring the implementation of programs and projects to ensure the timely implementation/completion thereof.

We recommended and  the Local Chief Executive agreed to  instruct the MDRRM Officer to (a) strictly comply with budgeting requirement on the allocation of the 5% LDRRMF as to the 70% allocation for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery fund and 30% lump-sum allocation for QRF; (b) monitor and ensure the implementation of the DRRM-related PPAs; (c) coordinate with the Municipal Planning Development Office to ensure that all approved DRRM-related PPAs are included in the AIP; (d) prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plan for current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance; and (e) furnish the OCD with the copy of the LDRRMFIP and monthly utilization reports in accordance with the requirements of COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

We commended the Municipal Environmental and Natural Resources Officer for devising ways to improve the operational capability of the municipal MRF resulting in the reduction in the volume of garbage and waste materials for final disposal and provision of additional income to MRF personnel.

Further, we recommended that the MENR Officer continue to adhere with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 for the effective implementation of solid waste management activities in the Municipality.

We recommended and  the Local Chief Executive, as chairman of the GFPS,  agreed to instruct the members, the GAD Technical Working Group (TWG) and GAD Focal Person to (a) continue the GFPS’ compliance with the requirements on the institutionalization of GAD mechanisms and mandate of promoting a gender-responsive environment in the local government; and (b) in coordination with other municipal offices, to ensure that the GAD Plan and Budget and the submitted and maintained GAD reports and records are properly prepared and reconciled.

We recommended that the Municipal Administrator cause the submission of the lacking documents, original copy of the survey plan, and copy of the Transfer Certificate of Title under the name of the Municipality certified by the Register of Deeds for the purchase of the four parcels of land pursuant to the requirements of COA Circular 2012-001, to fully substantiate the regularity of the transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MENRO to (a) submit the lacking documents for the purchase of the parcel of land pursuant to the requirements of COA Circular 2012-001; and (b) coordinate with the proper government agencies and cause the proper titling of the purchased parcel of land.

We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) agreed to strictly observe the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001 on the process/procedures in the receipt and opening of bids, evaluation of bids, post-qualification of the lowest calculated bid, preparation of contract and issuance of Notice to Proceed and completeness of the documents supporting the procurement activities, contracts and disbursement vouchers.

Further, we recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to, in coordination with other municipal offices/units, (a) submit the lacking documents; and (b) henceforth, ensure that all payments to contractors are properly supported with the required documents.

Moreover, we recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to (a) remind the contractors to cause the implementation of the necessary preventive measures such as the provision of PPE and imposition of wearing the same during project implementation to eliminate or minimize the exposure of their workers to hazard; and (b) observe the provisions of the Revised IRR on the appropriate period of accepting the completed projects to maximize the time that the projects are covered with warranty.

Hence, we reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive, instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to (a) closely monitor the implementation of all projects; and (b) regularly check the condition of completed projects for early detection of defects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Business Permits and Licensing Office (BPLO) to (i) apply the appropriate categories for business establishments; and (ii) levy and collect taxes in compliance with the Municipality’s Revenue Code; (b) Municipal Treasurer's Office (MTO) and other concerned offices to revise the Revenue Code in order to clearly express the intention of the Municipal Government to arrive at higher taxes; (c) BPLO and MTO to require the submission of sworn statement of gross receipts or sales and certified copies of income tax returns so as to come-up with an accurate basis for the computation of business taxes; (d) BPLO and MTO to verify, assess and validate the noted under collection of ₱115,939.97 and consequently demand the collection/payment thereof; (e) the concerned municipal officials and employees to submit a written explanation on the noted deficiencies.

We recommended  and Management agreed to direct (a) continuous  compliance  with the provisions on the regular and full remittance within the prescribed period for the withheld taxes, GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth premiums/contributions and loan amortizations; and (b) the   Municipal Accounting Office to review and reconcile the outstanding balances of the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts and recognize the adjusting entries, if necessary, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated the following recommendations that the:
(a)	Municipal Treasurer 
(i) at the end of every month, reconcile the cashbook balances with accounting records; and 
(ii) make the necessary adjustments;
(b)	OIC-Municipal Accountant draw the necessary journal entry to reflect the correct balance of the cash accounts; and
(c)	OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer closely coordinate and regularly reconcile their respective records for the early detection and correction of errors.

observations.recommendation:
1.9. We reiterated the following recommendations that the:
(a) Municipal Treasurer (i) at the end of every month, reconcile the cashbook
balances with accounting records; and (ii) make the necessary adjustments;
(b) OIC-Municipal Accountant draw the necessary journal entry to reflect the
correct balance of the cash accounts; and
(c) OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer closely coordinate and
regularly reconcile their respective records for the early detection and
correction of errors.

2.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal
Treasurer to furnish the OIC-Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a
duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible
for the year which shall serve as the basis for the setting-up of the RPT and SET
receivables due for the year; and (b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer and OIC-
Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation of records to establish a
realistic balance of RPT and SET Receivables so that the necessary adjustment(s) in
the books of accounts can be effected for the fair presentation of balances in the
financial statements.

3.5. We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant make the necessary
adjusting entry to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.

4.13. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory
Committee to facilitate the conduct of the physical count of all inventory items
semestrally to establish the correct balances of each account and submit a complete
and accurate Report on the Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI), as required.
4.14. Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all
department heads and designated supply officers to (a) formulate policies and
procedures in the control and monitoring of all inventories purchased and issued; (b)
ensure the preparation and timely submission of reports pertaining to the issuance of
inventory items; and (c) strictly comply with the rules and regulations contained in
the NGAS Manual for the proper recording, valuation and preparation of the
required inventory reports.

5.26. We recommended that the:
(a) Inventory Committee (i) conduct a complete actual physical inventory
count of all the PPEs owned by the Municipality pursuant to COA Circular
2020-006; (ii) exert more effort in securing the needed documents or
information in the recognition and valuation of the donated PPEs
presented above, otherwise record the same in accordance with the
provisions of COA Circular 2020-006; and (iii) coordinate with the
Accounting Department the reconciliation of accounting records with the
RPCPPE for identification of differences in the PPE;
(b) Municipal Treasurer, together with the Municipal Assessor, facilitate the
titling of the 24 parcels of land already located and exert extra effort in
locating the remaining 78 parcels owned by the Municipality;
(c) OIC-Municipal Accountant (a) compute and provide depreciation for the
Road Networks asset account; and (b) make necessary adjusting entries to
reflect the correct balances of the PPE accounts;
(d) OIC-Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Engineer and the
Municipal Treasurer, initiate the proper recognition and inventory of the
Local Road Network System as well as the drafting of the Local Road
Network Map of the Municipality; and
(e) Local Chief Executive instruct all department heads account for all
donated PPEs under their custody and submit to the Accounting
Department all documents pertaining thereto for proper recognition in the
books.

6.7. We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant:
(a) conduct a detailed review of the Accounts Payable to ensure that only valid
obligations of the Municipality are reflected in the financial statements;
(b) prepare a Schedule of Accounts Payable which includes all the necessary
information such as name and address of the creditor, date the obligation
was incurred and aging of the accounts; and
(c) after thorough review, revert the excess payables and those which had
been outstanding for more than two years and without actual claims,
administrative or judicial, or which were not covered by perfected
contracts or records, if any.

7.23. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as Chairman of the
Municipal Development Council, instruct the members and secretariat of the MDC
and implementing departments to ensure (a) that development projects to be funded
out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement and implementation ready; (b)
timely completion and full implementation of the projects, at the same time, optimize
the use of the fund to guarantee timely enjoyment of the benefits that the projects
may provide to the intended beneficiaries.

8.3. In view of the foregoing observations and based on the information gathered,
we recommended that the Local Chief Executive, in accordance with the provisions
of the MOA, designate a staff as project coordinator who will regularly monitor the
utilization of funds and implementation of the project.

9.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct all implementing
departments to monitor the implementation of programs and projects to ensure the
timely implementation/completion thereof.

10.18. We recommended that the MDRRM Officer (a) incorporate the unexpended
balance of the LDRRMF under the Special Trust Fund in the preparation of
theLDRRMF Investment Plan/LDRRMF Plan to be integrated in the AIP; and (b)
submit supplemental/amended AIP showing the changes in current year
appropriations such as realignments.
10.19. Further, we recommended that the MDRRM Officer and Municipal
Accountant (a) ensure that all DRRMF are included in the Report on Utilization of
DRRMF for easy monitoring of the utilization of the fund; and (b) prepare all
required reports pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

11.12. We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive, as
Chairman of the LSB, the Co-Chairman and other members of the LSB (a) conduct
consultation/discussion with heads of schools and other stakeholders for strategic
prioritization in the allocation of the SEF and to properly plan the PPAs to be
implemented from the fund; and (b) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer,
Municipal Budget Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant for the available SEF and
maximize the utilization of the Fund to attain its goals and objectives.

12.20. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GAD Focal
Person to continue its (a) compliance with the certain requirements on the
institutionalization of GAD mechanisms; and (b) mandate of promoting a gender-
responsive environment in the local government.
12.21. For the unfavorable observations noted, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive, as Chairman of the GFPS, instruct the GFPS Technical Working Group,
in coordination with other municipal offices, to (a) monitor the implementation of
GAD-related PPAs; and (b) ensure that the submitted and maintained GAD reports
and records are properly prepared and reconciled.

13.22. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly
observe the provisions of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001, especially on (a)
the completeness of the documents supporting the procurement activities, contract
and disbursement vouchers; and (b) evaluation of bidding documents.
13.23. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office ensure that
the required detailed engineering activities are carried out properly to guarantee the
correct computation of the project requirements.
13.24. Moreover, we recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant, in
coordination with the Municipal Engineer, ensure that all payments to contractors
are properly supported with the required documents.

14.11. We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Engineer, submit
an explanation on the noted undue payment for uninstalled/unused pay items.
14.12. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office, as
implementing department for infrastructure projects, ensure that contractors comply
with the posting requirements of COA Circular 2013-004.

15.8. Hence, we reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive,
instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to (a) closely monitor the implementation
of all projects; (b) create a monitoring team that will regularly check the condition of
completed projects for early detection of defects; and (c) require the Contractor
concerned to perform the appropriate repair of the portion of the projects found to
be defective at no additional cost to the Municipality.

16.18. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, instruct the Municipal
Health Officer to (a) prepare and submit to the Audit Team a detailed record of the
received and issued COVID-19 vaccines by the Rural Health Unit 2; and (b)
henceforth, ensure that the information recorded for the receipt, issuance and
balances of COVID-19 vaccines are complete, accurate and detailed.

17.7. We recommended that the Management (a) conduct intensive tax campaigns,
employ collection strategies, and institute legal remedies pursuant to the pertinent
provisions of RA No. 7160 in order to persuade the taxpayers to pay their taxes on
time; and (b) enforce the collection of the unpaid delinquent taxes and formulate
policies to standardize the giving of tax incentives or relief to delinquent taxpayers.

18.18. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Human Resource
Management Officer to:
(a) continue to conduct a thorough review of its existing organizational
structure in order to properly identify and establish its appropriate
human resource complement and creation of additional permanent
position which may be considered for regular functions of the agency;
(b) hire job order personnel based on necessity and perform periodic
performance evaluation and assessment of Job Order personnel to
ensure value for money and avoid wastage of government funds by
paying compensation to JOs who do not actually provide service, or
whose services rendered do not commensurate to their compensation;
(c) require JO personnel to properly accomplish their DTRs to reflect the
actual number of days and time rendered and submit actual and
quantified accomplishment reports to support payment of wages;
(d) submit a written explanation on the noted differences and deficiencies
on compensation/wages of JO personnel;
(e) refer to the provisions of CSC-DBM Circular No. 1, s. 2018, CSC-COA-
DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017, Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised
Omnibus Rules on Appointments and Other Personnel Actions and
CSC Memorandum Circular No. 24, series of 2002 regarding the policy
guidelines on the hiring, supervision and payment of JO personnel.

19.10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MENRO to (a)
account for and record all the succeeding receipt thereof from RCBM and the
eventual utilization; (b) develop operating standards to monitor the receipt and
issuance of the cements and to ensure that these will be used only for projects that
support the Municipality’s solid waste management programs; and (d) ensure that
the same are properly safeguarded against theft and spoilage.

20.7. However, we recommended that the Accounting Department coordinate with
the Government Service Insurance System, Home Development Mutual Fund, and
Philippine Health Insurance Corporation and request reconciliation of the
outstanding balance of the Due to GSIS, HDMF and PhilHealth which claimed to be
pertaining to transactions in the previous years to determine if these are valid
obligations of the Municipality and immediately remit the same, if warranted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, instruct the Municipal Health Office to (a) coordinate with the Provincial Government and Department of Health Regional Office No. III for the noted difference on the transferred vaccines; (b) conduct thorough review of the masterlist of vaccinated individuals and make the necessary corrections; (c) prepare and submit to the Audit Team a detailed record of received and issued vaccines; and (d) henceforth, ensure that the information reflected on records and reports are complete, correct and accurate.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer closely monitors the implementation of all infrastructure projects to avoid similar errors in the future.

Of the 18 projects found defective and/or not compliant with the project requirements that necessitate maintenance, improvement, repair and/or reconstruction, specifically roads/core local roads, 16 projects were repaired. The Asphalt Overlay, Along Provincial Road, San Pedro 2nd Phase was coordinated to the Contractor and is now completed. As for the Construction of Drainage System Coronel Farm to Riverbank (Gumain River) Cabangcalan, the variation order for the project was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council conduct thorough evaluation and planning of the DRRM PPAs for effective and efficient utilization of the LDRRMF. Moreover, we recommended that LDRRM Officer (a) ensure that all DRRMF are included on the Report on Utilization of DRRMF for easy monitoring of the utilization of the fund; and (b) prepare all required reports pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as chairman of the Chairman of the GFPS, instruct the GFPS Technical Working Group, in coordination with other municipal offices, to (a) ensure that at least five percent of the budget are allocated/attributed for GAD-related PPAs; (b) monitor the implementation of GAD-related PPAs; and (c) ensure that the submitted and maintained GAD reports and records are properly prepared and reconciled. Further, we recommended that Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to exert effort to complete the GAD database to ensure that identified GAD PPAs programmed to be implemented are gender-responsive.
observations.managementAction:
•	To closely monitor the implementation of the GAD related PPAs, the Local Chief Executive issued EO No. 45-2021, designating the GAD Focal Person who shall implement, coordinate and monitor the GAD Action Plan. Aside from that, the LCE instructed the GFPS to properly monitor the implementation of the GAD related PPAs, actual utilization of the GAD Fund must be presented during the quarterly GFPS meeting to reconcile the utilization of the different departments with the utilization recorded by the Budget Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, instruct the concerned municipal offices to (a) stop paying financial assistance through petty cash fund; (b) henceforth, strictly comply with the rules and guidelines in the grant of financial assistance particularly on the required supporting documents; and (c) devise a coordinated system in the assessment, evaluation and grant of financial assistance to ensure that all the beneficiaries are eligible, the transactions are properly documented, the assistance are actually received by the beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Management hire job order personnel based on necessity and perform periodic performance evaluation and assessment thereon to ensure value for money and avoid wastage of government funds through payment of compensation to JOs who do not actually provide service, or whose services rendered do not commensurate to their compensation. Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive, instruct the Human Resource Management Officer to (a) initiate the conduct of the review of its existing organizational structure in order to properly identify and establish the Municipality’s appropriate human resource complement and create additional permanent position which may be considered for regular functions of the agency; (b) require JO personnel to properly accomplish their DTRs to reflect the actual number of days and time rendered and submit quantified accomplishment reports to support payment of wages; (c) refer to the provisions of Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised Omnibus Rules on Appointments and Other Personnel Actions and CSC Memorandum Circular No. 24, series of 2002 regarding the policy guidelines on the hiring, supervision, and payment of JO personnel.
observations.managementAction: The Management reminded department heads and supervisors to monitor all JO personnel under their supervision to strictly assess their performances and also the necessity of hiring them.

Inventory Committee to

Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the 
(i) Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated file of property cards; and 
(ii) Municipal Assessor to ascertain the status of parcels of land with titling issues and facilitate the titling thereof in the name of the Municipality of Floridablanca; and
Municipal Accountant to 
(i) determine the PPE for reclassification to inventory accounts for the correct valuation of the PPE accounts; 
(ii) draw a journal entry recognizing the titled parcel of land in the books of the Municipality; and 
(iii) prepare a lapsing schedule for the depreciation of the Other Land Improvements and Infrastructure Assets pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008 and Section 262 of the GAM for LGUs.

We recommended and Management committed that the 
Municipal Accountant, along with the other offices/officials concerned, comply with the submission of the lacking documents; and
 concerned personnel submit satisfactory explanations on the deficiencies noted on the submitted documents for the evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, instruct the Municipal Health Office to (a) coordinate with the Provincial Government and DOH RO III for the noted difference on the transferred vaccines; (b) conduct thorough review of the masterlist of vaccinated individuals and make the necessary corrections; (c) prepare and submit to the Audit Team a detailed record of received and issued vaccines; and (d) henceforth, ensure that the information reflected on records and reports are complete, correct and accurate.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) at the end of every month, reconcile the cashbook balances with accounting records; (b) make the necessary adjustments in the cashbooks; and (c) report immediately all issued checks in the RCI for the accurate reporting of the Cash in Bank account balance.

We also recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant draw the necessary journal entry to reflect the correct balance of the cash accounts.

Further, we recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer closely coordinate and regularly reconcile their respective records for the early detection and correction of errors.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the concerned accountable personnel to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purposes were served, and no additional cash advances shall be granted unless the previous ones were liquidated or settled; and (b) require explanation on the delayed liquidation of the funds.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee (a) conduct a complete physical inventory count of all the PPE owned by the Municipality pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006; (b) exert more effort in securing the needed documents or information in the recognition and valuation of the donated PPEs presented above; and (c) coordinate with the Accounting Department for the reconciliation of the PPE reported in RPCPPE and the accounting records for the identification of difference and make the necessary adjustments in the RPCPPE, if needed.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Assessor (a) coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer for the provision of funds for the titling/transfer of the 24 parcels of land; and (b) exert more effort in locating the remaining 78 parcels of land.

Moreover, we recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant (a) submit the details of the reclassified PPE totaling ₱3,330,727.26; (b) compute and provide depreciation for the Land Improvements and Infrastructure Assets: and (c) make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant (a) in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, conduct thorough review of all outstanding payables (b) prepare a Schedule of Accounts Payable which includes all the necessary information such as name and address of the creditor, date the obligation was incurred and aging of the account; and (c) revert the excess payables and those which had been outstanding for more than two years and without actual claims, administrative or judicial, or which were not covered by perfected contracts or records.

For the noted deficiencies, we recommended and the Local Chief Executive instructed the Acting Municipal Accountant to (a) conduct thorough review of the composition of the affected accounts to determine the circumstances of transactions; (b) coordinate with the source agency for the proper treatment of the received funds in the books; and (c) make the necessary journal entries.

We commended the Municipal Development Council, chaired by the Local Chief Executive, and recommended that it continue to strengthen the cycle of planning, execution, monitoring and evaluation of programs and projects for prioritization under the 20% DF in order to ensure well prepared, and implementation-ready programs and projects.

We reiterated our recommendation and the secretariat of the Municipal Development Council, Bids and Awards Committee, and Municipal Engineer, committed to include in the AIP and APP the specific period of implementation of the PPAs for the ensuing year to ensure timely implementation of the projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer and other implementing departments to monitor the implementation of programs and projects to ensure timely completion and avoid non-compliance with the conditions of the transfers.

In view of the foregoing observations and based on the information gathered, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive, (a) through the Municipal Agriculturist, demand from RiceUp Farmers Incorporated the physical and financial reports and Fund Utilization Report for the implementation of the Enhanced Kadiwa program; (b) instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to conduct a thorough assessment of the implementation of the project, in consideration of the appeal of the farmer group-beneficiaries; and (c) make the necessary actions pursuant to the conditions of the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, instruct the Municipal Engineer’s Office to (a) closely monitor the implementation of all projects; (b) create monitoring team that will regularly check the condition of completed projects for early detection of defects; (c) require the Contractor concerned to cause the repair of the portion of the projects found to be defective at no additional cost to the Municipality; and (d) for the deficient projects, require the Contractor concerned to submit an explanation and demand the completion of the projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Accountant

require the officers and employees (i) deemed liable for the settlement of the total amount of ₱2,201,200.31 corresponding to the gasoline issued to private vehicles; and (ii) responsible for the submission of the driver’s trip tickets for gasoline expenses amounting to ₱24,673.88;

henceforth, (a) refrain from (i) issuing gasoline for privately-owned vehicles; and (ii) allowing the gasoline reimbursements of officials and department heads without the driver’s trip tickets; and (b) require all officials and personnel concerned with assigned government vehicle to properly and fully accomplish the Driver’s Trip Tickets; and


strictly comply with the provision of BIR Revenue Memorandum Order (RMO) No. 8-2003 as reiterated under Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012 on the proper withholding of taxes on payments to suppliers.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2017 | Based on the sampled transactions for the last quarter of CY 2018, substantially, the Municipality had (i) refrained from issuing gasoline to privately-owned motor vehicles and as showed by the minimal decreased of the recorded Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expenses in the FS amounting to ₱11,952,512.49 in CY 2018 as compared to ₱12,001,633.30 recorded in CY 2017; and (ii) also complied that payments for gasoline expenses were supported with fully accomplished driver’s trip tickets.

The Municipality substantially complied with the applicable tax laws and other tax-related government issuances on the deduction and withholding of taxes on payments to suppliers.
observations.reason: The liable officers and employees did not (i) settle the corresponding gasoline expenses issued to private vehicles; and (ii) submit the lacking driver’s trip tickets.

observations.recommendation: 2.5	We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to report without delay the aforementioned checks in the RCI and forward the same, together with the disbursement vouchers and the supporting documents, to the Municipal Accountant for the recording thereof in the Journals of Checks Issued. Henceforth, report immediately all issued checks in the RCI for the accurate reporting of the account balance.
3.4	We recommended and the Management agreed to comply with the submission of the lacking documents/information as listed in Annexes E1 and E2 for the evaluation of the Audit Team.

4.15	We recommended and the Management agreed (i) to implement programs, projects and activities in the 20% Development Fund which are in the nature of high-impact investment or capital expenditures as envisioned by the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DILG and DBM; (ii) that the Municipal Local Development Council conduct an in-depth planning for prioritization of  PPAs in the 20% DF by the consensus of the constituents and coordination with other LGUs/agencies to avoid realignment and sudden change of projects intended for current year’s implementation; and (iii) to require the Municipal Engineer and other concerned heads of implementing offices improve the implementation of the proposed project through judicious and early planning.

5.12	We recommended that the (a) LDRRM Officer include in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years and prepare the required PPMP for consolidation in the APP; (b) Municipal Budget Officer see to it that all charges in the LDRRMF are within the approved budget and avoid the incurrence of appropriations overdraft; (c) MDRRMC organize and conduct trainings, orientations and knowledge management activities on disaster risk reduction and management on a continuing basis to maintain and provide suitably-trained and competent personnel for effective emergency operations and responses to all types of disasters; (d) Municipal Accountant follow the accounting guidelines on the recording of procurements to the appropriate Inventory account, and together with the Requisitioning Officer install an effective internal control in the recording of receipts and issuances by maintaining the supplies ledger cards and stock cards, respectively; and (e) Requisitioning Office submit the list of recipients indicating the quantity and particularity of the welfare goods and supplies issued for proper accounting thereof.

6.7	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MENR Officer to (a) utilize the newly constructed structures/MRF at Barangay Mabical, and the one at Barangay Palmayo Resettlement once completed, for the systematic transfer and storage of residual wastes and reducing the expenditures of the Municipality caused by using the transfer station; and (b) further introduce effective projects and programs to support the Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program to reduce the volume of garbage waste materials and decrease the amount of tipping fees being paid to Metro Clark Waste Management Corporation.
7.6	We recommended and the Management agreed to continue being compliant with the provisions on the withholding and timely remittance of taxes set forth in BIR RMC No. 23-2012.

8.6	We recommended that the (a) Management continue to being compliant with the provisions on the timely remittance of Pag-IBIG contributions and loan amortizations of permanent employees within the prescribed periods; (b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer address the issue on the noted delays on the remittances of Pag-IBIG contributions of job orders; and (c) Municipal Accountant analyze the Due to Pag-IBIG account to trace the unremitted amount of ₱49,753.50.

9.4	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to trace the unremitted amount of Ᵽ529,210.94 for the correct presentation of the account balance in the financial statements and remit the same, if necessary.

10.12	We recommended that the (a) Municipal Treasurer, and concerned personnel of the Accounting and HRM Offices familiarize and continuously update themselves with applicable PhilHealth issued circulars and memoranda to ensure the correct and timely remittances of PhilHealth premium contributions particularly those from job orders; and (b) Municipal Accountant analyze the Due to PhilHealth account to trace the unremitted amount of Ᵽ250,093.61.

11.11	We recommended that the Management (a) reduce the number of the job orders particularly those (i) without specific work performed; (ii) with excessive numbers performing similar work; and (iii) with functions overlapping with those of the permanent employees, to minimize the expenses of the Municipality and to comply with the PS limitation as provided for in Section 325 of RA 7160; and (b) require the concerned JOs to refund the double payment totaling ₱195,300.00.

12.6	We recommended that the Management (a) strictly observe economy measures in the disposition of the Municipality’s funds and resources, maximize the existing manpower complement, and determine the need for consultancy on cases where the regular plantilla personnel does not have the capacity to provide; and (b) prepare a written policy on the rates of consultants’ services.

13.8	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Planning and Development Officer to facilitate the completion of the CBMS to have sufficient information in significantly addressing the gender issues or gaps within the locality which should be based on the results of gender audit and analysis using sex-disaggregated data; (b) GAD Focal Person to monitor and improve the implementation of the GAD PPAs for the benefit of the intended recipients/beneficiaries; and (c) Municipal Budget Officer see to it that all charges in the GAD Fund are within the approved plan and budget and avoid the incurrence of appropriations overdraft.

14.10	We recommended that the members of the Municipal Local School Board (a) allocate and utilize the SEF budget for high-priority and eligible PPAs under the latest SEF guidelines and consider high-impact and laudable investment/capital expenditures projects instead of generally reprogramming repairs and maintenance of school buildings and other facilities; and (b) prepare the required PPMP, and APP at the start of the budget period.

We recommended that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board (a) 		prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and (b) request for inputs from the Deped official/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) continue to invest in high-impact capital expenditures projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund  and refrain from proposing non-capital investment projects under the Fund;  and (b) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

We commended the Municipal Accountant for its compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMP as incorporated in the local development plans and annual work and financial plan to ensure the optimum implementation of the pre-disaster preparedness programs.  However we recommended that management carefully plan their disaster preparedness programs/projects/activities to achieve their desired goals.

2.7    We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) prioritize the allocation of funds under the 20% Development Fund for the establishment of another structure or MRF at Sitio Palakol, San Jose to pave for the systematic transfer and storing of compostable residual wastes; and (b) enforce strictly the no segregation, no collection policy and the no-littering policy and intensify information campaign among the residents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require  (a) all officials and personnel  concerned with assigned government vehicle to properly and fully accomplish the Driver’s Trip Tickets and submit a Summary of the Monthly Report of Official Travels at the end month to the designated GSO ; and (b) the designated GSO to  submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption together with the Report of Official Travels to the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the succeeding month to determine the validity and reasonableness of gas, oil and lubricants expenditures.

We recommended that the (a) BAC Secretariat include the procurement of common-use supplies from the PS-DBM in the Municipality’s Annual Procurement Plan; and (b) General Services Officer procure its common-use supplies at the PS-DBM and employ the method of shopping only when conditions are satisfied in accordance with the provisions of Section 52.1 of the RIRR.
